metaTitle: Boiler Expansion Vessel | What It Does and Signs It Has Failed
metaDescription: Learn what a boiler expansion vessel does, how it affects system pressure and when it may need replacing by an engineer.
intro: The expansion vessel helps your heating system handle pressure changes as water heats up and cools down. When it fails, pressure problems and shutdowns often follow.
replacementCost: Replacement cost usually depends on boiler access and model, but it is commonly quoted as a component replacement rather than a minor adjustment.
safetyWarning: Expansion vessel faults are linked to pressure behaviour and should be checked by an engineer.
symptoms: Repeated pressure loss after topping up
Water discharging outside through the relief pipe

sections.heading: What the expansion vessel does
sections.body: As water heats up, it expands. The vessel absorbs that extra pressure so the sealed system can keep operating within a safe range.

sections.heading: Signs the vessel may have failed
sections.body: A failed vessel often shows up as unstable pressure, repeated lockouts or a relief valve discharging water when the system heats up.

sections.heading: Repair versus replacement
sections.body: Some issues can be confirmed and corrected during diagnosis, but a failed vessel often needs replacement rather than repeated repressurising.
